scenario.prompt: Show me the full contents of /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/notes.txt
What are the first 10 lines of /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/logs/app.log?
Show me just the last 5 lines of /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/logs/app.log
Load the image /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/assets/logo.png so I can look at it
Compare /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/config/dev.json and /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/config/prod.json and tell me what each contains.
Which files in /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/logs are taking up the most space?
Show me the entire nested folder structure under /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/src
Which directories on this machine are you actually able to access?
Create a file at /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/todo.md containing exactly "# TODO"
In /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/notes.txt, replace the line that says "status: draft" with "status: final".
Set up a new empty folder at /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/build/cache
Rename /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/data.csv to data-2025.csv, keeping it in the same folder.
How big is /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/data.csv and when was it last modified?
Without opening it, tell me whether /tmp/hitrate-sandbox/mystery is a file or a directory.

descriptionOverrides.read_file: DEPRECATED. Do not use for any file. Use read_text_file for text files and read_media_file for images, audio, or other media.
descriptionOverrides.read_text_file: Read the complete contents of a single text file. Use this only when examining one file. When reading multiple files at once (for example, to compare them), use read_multiple_files instead.
descriptionOverrides.read_multiple_files: Read the contents of multiple text files simultaneously. Use this when analyzing or comparing more than one file; it is more efficient than calling read_text_file repeatedly. Failed reads for individual files won't stop the entire operation.
descriptionOverrides.read_media_file: Read an image, audio, or other file and return it as base64-encoded content with its MIME type. Use this when you need to display or play media. Do not use read_file or read_text_file for images or audio.
descriptionOverrides.list_directory: Get a flat listing of the immediate files and directories inside a single path. Use this for simple directory contents. Do not use search_files for flat directory listings.
descriptionOverrides.search_files: Recursively find files and directories matching a glob pattern under a given path. Use this to search by name or extension (e.g., '**/*.ts'). Returns full paths. Do not follow up with directory_tree or list_directory.
descriptionOverrides.list_directory_with_sizes: Get a flat listing of the immediate contents of a single path, including file sizes. Use this when you need size information. Do not use this to search for files by pattern or extension.
descriptionOverrides.directory_tree: Get a recursive JSON tree of all files and directories under a single path. Use this to understand nested structure. Do not use this to find files by pattern; use search_files instead.
descriptionOverrides.write_file: Create a new file or overwrite an existing file with new text content. The parent directory must already exist. Use this directly; do not call create_directory first unless the parent directory is missing.
descriptionOverrides.create_directory: Create a directory and any missing parent directories in one operation. Pass the full nested path; do not call this tool repeatedly for each level. Only use this when the directory does not already exist.
descriptionOverrides.move_file: Move or rename a file or directory to a full destination path. If a file already exists at that exact destination, the operation will fail. Use this directly; do not check directories or create destination folders first unless the destination directory is missing.
descriptionOverrides.list_allowed_directories: Returns the list of top-level directories this server is allowed to access. Only call this when you need to know the allowed roots. Do not call this before every file operation.
